title: Thelypteris iridescens
intro: Thelypteris iridescens, also known as the Iridescent Lady Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae, characterized by its delicate fronds and iridescent scales. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris iridescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Thelypteris iridescens belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns. It is also classified under the order Polypodiales, which includes a large number of fern species. The genus Thelypteris is a part of the subclass Polypodiidae, which is a subgroup of the class Polypodiopsida. The class Polypodiopsida is a division of vascular plants that includes ferns and their allies. Thelypteris iridescens is further classified under the kingdom Plantae, which encompasses a wide range of plant species.
